location.office_time_required: blend of in-office and remote work (specifics not stated)

level_signals: 6-8+ years of experience in high-performance cultures
1+ years of experience managing high-performing teams
Comp range $136K-$200K base, consistent with a manager-level role at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6-8+ years of experience. You've succeeded in high-performance cultures (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience).
You are a leader. You have 1+ years of experience managing high-performing teams.
You're analytical. You let data win arguments, and you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL or modeling in Excel.
You solve problems from first principles. You're excited to solve problems in innovative ways, and you resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environments
You have completed your BS/BA Degree.

you will lead a team of 2 associates and own high-impact initiatives from end to end—including developing the initial strategy, executing with precision, and analyzing results to inform future decisions.
You will own annual volume planning and forecasting processes, and deliver weekly performance reporting with clear insights and actionable recommendations.
You'll collaborate closely with cross-functional partners in product, category management, and finance to identify and scale growth levers.

scope.surface: DashMart — convenience store / quick-commerce business; growth strategy, customer conversion, personalized shopper experiences, in-app merchandising, occasions strategies
core_responsibilities: Lead a team of 2 associates and own high-impact initiatives end to end
Develop initial strategy, execute with precision, and analyze results
Own annual volume planning and forecasting processes
Deliver weekly performance reporting with clear insights and actionable recommendations
Collaborate with product, category management, and finance to identify and scale growth levers
Drive customer conversion through personalized shopper experiences, in-app merchandising, and occasions strategies
Transform strategies into operational experiments with measurable outcomes

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($136K-$200K base) at the national level
Equity grants mentioned as part of compensation
Clear team size (2 direct reports) and reporting structure (Director of DashMart Growth Strategy & Operations)
Specific and tangible ownership areas (annual volume planning, weekly reporting, conversion initiatives)
Strong mission signal — building DoorDash's 'next billion dollar business'
Comprehensive benefits package including 401(k) with employer match, 16 weeks parental leave, wellness benefits
red_flags: Compensation range is very wide ($136K-$200K, a $64K spread), making it difficult to gauge target pay without further recruiter clarification
No specific cities listed for hybrid requirement; unclear which offices are applicable
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
8+ years upper bound on experience range is ambiguous — could exclude candidates with 9-10+ years if role is scoped lower

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years requirement, and direct report count)

location.cities_listed (not specified for this role; left empty)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'hybrid' description and 'remote jobs associated with office in NYC' notice)
ambiguities: JD says 'hybrid role, with a blend of in-office and remote work' but does not specify which city/office or minimum in-office frequency
Years of experience stated as '6-8+ years' — the '+' after 8 suggests open-ended, but the framing could also be read as targeting a specific range; treated as minimum of 6 here
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; left as 'unclear'
